strengths: 20 years of continuous software development experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains, meeting the 15-year minimum requirement.
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling at re:cinq demonstrates direct engagement with agentic AI workflows.
Demonstrated ability to build and ship full products independently (GISCollective, CMSushi, Fluent-Asserts), showing builder fluency across the stack.
concerns: AI experience is entirely superficial and recent — limited to 3 months of MCP tooling at re:cinq with zero prior ML/AI track record, far below the 'recognized expertise with demonstrated AI products or platforms' the role demands.

Career trajectory peaks at Staff/Senior Software Developer with no evidence of director-level scope, strategic advisory work, customer-facing thought leadership, or influencing organizational engineering direction — core to this principal role.

No advanced degree (preferred Master's/PhD in AI) and no recognition in AI or cloud communities beyond local workshops and a single FOSS4G talk; the role requires externally recognized expertise to credibly advise strategic customers and partners.

key_factors.factor: Cloud and AI strategic advisory track record

response.justification: This candidate is a competent generalist software developer with broad technical range but is misaligned with a director-level AI strategy role in almost every dimension that matters: they have never operated at director scope, have no history of advising enterprise customers or shaping cloud/AI product strategy, and their only AI experience is three months of MCP tooling — a thin credential against a requirement for recognized, demonstrated AI expertise. The role calls for someone who can externally inspire and advise on cutting-edge AI adoption; this resume reflects an execution-focused individual contributor, not a principal-level technology strategist.
